title: Bart The Murderer

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase that became cultural phenomenon. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: You ate my homework? I didn't know dogs really did that.
jokes.setup: The classic 'dog ate my homework' excuse as cliche
jokes.punchline: Bart's surprise that it actually happens in real life
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the tired excuse, well-structured observational humor

jokes.text: But I'm only five... 10, 20... 40 minutes? That's pretty damn late.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to minimize his tardiness
jokes.punchline: The escalating realization of how late he actually is
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing Bart's self-awareness and acceptance

jokes.text: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!
jokes.setup: Bart realizes he forgot his permission slip for the chocolate factory trip
jokes.punchline: Extended scream of despair
jokes.explanation: Classic cartoon despair reaction, well-timed but predictable

jokes.text: Here's a whole box of unsealed envelopes for the P.T.A. You're making me lick envelopes?
jokes.setup: Skinner presents envelope licking as Bart's punishment activity
jokes.punchline: Bart's incredulous reaction to the mundane punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner bureaucratic punishment, captures his character well

jokes.text: Licking envelopes can be fun. Just make a game of it. What kind of game? Well, see how many you can lick in an hour. Then try to break that record. Sounds like a pretty crappy game.
jokes.setup: Skinner's earnest attempt to make envelope licking sound fun
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assessment that it's a crappy game
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Skinner's delusional optimism vs Bart's honesty

jokes.text: I'm Troy McClure. You probably remember me from such films as The Revenge of Abe Lincoln and The Wackiest Covered Wagon in the West.
jokes.setup: Troy McClure's signature introduction formula
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake movie titles
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure format with perfectly absurd fake movie titles, became iconic

jokes.text: In those days... instead of being wrapped in a hygienic package... chocolate was wrapped in a tobacco leaf. Instead of being pure chocolate like we have today it was mixed with shredded tobacco and they didn't eat it--they smoked it.
jokes.setup: Educational documentary about chocolate history
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated 'history' that chocolate was smoked
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist fake history delivered with documentary authority

jokes.text: You didn't believe me when I said it would be fun, did you? No, thir.
jokes.setup: Skinner asking if envelope licking was fun as promised
jokes.punchline: Student's lisp from licking envelopes affecting speech
jokes.explanation: Clever physical consequence joke showing effects of envelope licking

jokes.text: Tan I doe now? What? 'Tan... I... doe... now?'
jokes.setup: Bart trying to ask if he can go
jokes.punchline: His speech is impaired from envelope licking adhesive
jokes.explanation: Continues the envelope licking consequence, good payoff

jokes.text: Aaaaah! Ugh!... Aah!... Unh!... Son of a b... Oof!. What next? Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Bart falling down stairs after leaving school
jokes.punchline: Series of impacts and his defeated 'what next' followed by more trouble
jokes.explanation: Classic cartoon physical comedy with good escalation structure

jokes.text: Pick a horse, kid. Shelbyville Downs, third race. Make it good. Eat my shorts. Eat My Shorts.
jokes.setup: Mobster asking Bart to pick a horse for betting
jokes.punchline: Bart's catchphrase becomes a horse name
jokes.explanation: Clever integration of Bart's catchphrase into the plot mechanism

jokes.text: Hey, wait a minute, you little punk! That's the fifth race. I said the third race. Don't have a cow. Don't Have a Cow in the third. Put a deuce on him.
jokes.setup: Mistake in the race number
jokes.punchline: Another Bart catchphrase becomes a horse name
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure using Bart's other catchphrase

jokes.text: It's Suffering Succotash by a neck... over Yabba-Dabba-Do, Ain't I a Stinker, and That's All Folks.
jokes.setup: Horse race announcement
jokes.punchline: All horses named after cartoon catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Continues the cartoon catchphrase horse theme established earlier

jokes.text: I like this kid. And we were gonna shoot him.
jokes.setup: Bart's horse pick wins
jokes.punchline: Casual mention they were going to kill a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivered with casual understatement, great character moment

jokes.text: Su-perb.
jokes.setup: Bart makes a Manhattan cocktail despite being a child
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's sophisticated approval
jokes.explanation: Good character voice, Fat Tony's pretentious approval of child bartender

jokes.text: What does it pay? Thirty bucks a week. I make more than that.
jokes.setup: Marge wants Homer to say something about Bart's job
jokes.punchline: Homer's only concern is the pay rate and his pride in earning more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing the point entirely and focusing on wrong thing

jokes.text: It's funny because it's true. Well observed.
jokes.setup: Characters commenting on something
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on comedy itself
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-joke but lacks context and character integration

jokes.text: What's a truck? Don't play dumb with me.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asks about cigarette truck hijacking
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's absurd feigning ignorance of what a truck is
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd criminal evasion tactic, perfectly economical

jokes.text: Bart! Have you started smoking? No. Don't lie to me, boy. Uh-huh! Cigarettes. Just as I thought. My boss said his warehouse was full. Yeah, right.
jokes.setup: Homer finds cigarettes and assumes Bart is smoking
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent explanation that his boss's warehouse is full
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and dramatic irony - audience knows the truth

jokes.text: I'm going to teach you a lesson. I'm going to stand here and watch you smoke every one of those cigarettes. Then maybe you'll learn.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to punish Bart for supposedly smoking
jokes.punchline: His punishment is forcing Bart to smoke more cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic failure - counterproductive parenting taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: You look good with that cigarette--kind of sophisticated. Son, I'll never doubt you again.
jokes.setup: Homer's punishment backfires
jokes.punchline: He's now proud of his son for smoking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - complete reversal from punishment to pride

jokes.text: the driver has been instructed to ignore all stop signs and crosswalks.
jokes.setup: Tobacco company spokesman reassuring about cigarette shortage
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dangerous illegal driving instructions
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate satire - casual disregard for public safety for profits

jokes.text: Fat Tony is a cancer on this fair city and I am the... Uh, what cures cancer?
jokes.setup: Wiggum making dramatic statement about Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know what cures cancer to complete his metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - starts confident metaphor but lacks knowledge to finish it

jokes.text: Bart, um... is it wrong to steal a loaf of bread to feed your starving family? No. For a large starving family is it wrong to steal a truckload of bread? Uh-uh. And what if your family don't like bread? They like... cigarettes. I guess that's okay.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony using Socratic method to justify crime
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity leading to cigarette theft justification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant criminal logic sequence, perfect escalation structure that becomes completely absurd

jokes.text: Give me three fingers of milk, Ma.
jokes.setup: Bart at home after working at mob bar
jokes.punchline: He orders milk using gangster terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing how Bart picks up mob mannerisms

jokes.text: If my plant pollutes the water and poisons the town by your logic, that would make me a criminal.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Bart working for criminals
jokes.punchline: His comparison accidentally admits to being a criminal himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic failure that accidentally reveals truth about his own job

jokes.text: Our cover's blown. Let's roll. See? It was all your imagination.
jokes.setup: Marge points out pizza truck has been there two weeks
jokes.punchline: Obvious surveillance team leaves immediately after being noticed
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy timing with Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: Read 'em and weep, boys. Another pair of sixes. Beats me. I was bluffing. You win again, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer playing poker with mobsters
jokes.punchline: He wins with terrible cards because he admits he was bluffing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer gambling incompetence - wins by being honest about bluffing

jokes.text: The only thing going on down there was male bonding. More stink lines, boys.
jokes.setup: Homer reports back to Marge about the club
jokes.punchline: Visual of Homer covered in stink lines while claiming it was just bonding
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy with Homer's obliviousness to obvious criminal activity

jokes.text: You didn't see nothing. Now, beat it.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers mobsters disposing of something
jokes.punchline: Standard mobster threat delivered to innocent Homer
jokes.explanation: Generic mobster dialogue - functional but predictable

jokes.text: What have I done to deserve this flat, flavorless Manhattan?
jokes.setup: Important guest tries Louie's Manhattan
jokes.punchline: Dramatic complaint about the terrible drink
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Louie's bartending incompetence setup

jokes.text: The kiss of death. That's all I need.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony sees the guest's displeasure
jokes.punchline: He recognizes this as serious mob terminology
jokes.explanation: Good mob terminology usage showing consequences of poor service

jokes.text: Some large men to see you, sir. I don't have an appointment with any large men.
jokes.setup: Secretary announcing visitors
jokes.punchline: Skinner's bureaucratic response to obvious threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner bureaucratic mindset even when facing obvious danger

jokes.text: And how did you get past the hall monitors?
jokes.setup: Mobsters have entered Skinner's office
jokes.punchline: His concern is about school security protocols
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Skinner moment - maintaining school administrator priorities even facing mobsters

jokes.text: I thought I found him, but it was only a cat.
jokes.setup: Search for missing Principal Skinner
jokes.punchline: Someone mistakes a cat for a person
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist joke about incompetent search efforts

jokes.text: I see wedding bells for Vanna White and Teddy Kennedy. If we could just stick to Principal Skinner. Willie Nelson will astound his fans by swimming the English Channel. Really? Willie Nelson?
jokes.setup: Psychic consultation about missing principal
jokes.punchline: Psychic gives random celebrity predictions instead, Wiggum gets distracted
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist fake psychic routine with Wiggum's gullibility

jokes.text: Hey! Look at me! I'm Skinner's body. That is not funny.
jokes.setup: Student making joke about missing/dead principal
jokes.punchline: Dark humor about corpse, teacher's appropriate response
jokes.explanation: Dark children's humor contrasted with adult disapproval

jokes.text: Hey! Look at me! I'm Skinner's body. / That is not funny. / I heard he's buried under his parking spot. / I heard he was ground into hamburger for lunch. / I heard Bart had him killed.
jokes.setup: Students gossiping about Skinner's disappearance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly morbid theories culminating in Bart as murderer
jokes.explanation: Dark school gossip escalation - kids' morbid imagination running wild

jokes.text: You're engaged in speculation. I know the law. You can't prove anything.
jokes.setup: Kids accusing Bart of having Skinner killed
jokes.punchline: Bart responds with legal terminology like a real criminal
jokes.explanation: Shows how Bart has learned criminal behavior and terminology

jokes.text: You killed me, Bart! You k-k-killed me, B-Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart's nightmare about killing Skinner
jokes.punchline: Ghost Skinner's accusation with stuttering effect
jokes.explanation: Standard nightmare sequence with spooky voice effect

jokes.text: Reverend Lovejoy! You've come to comfort me? / Yes, Bart. There, there. / Kill my boy.
jokes.setup: Bart seeks spiritual comfort from the reverend
jokes.punchline: Lovejoy's 'comfort' is telling Skinner's ghost to kill Bart
jokes.explanation: Dark misdirection - religious comfort turning into encouragement of violence

jokes.text: What's a murder?
jokes.setup: Police arrest them for murder
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony feigns ignorance of the word 'murder'
jokes.explanation: Callback to 'What's a truck?' - same criminal evasion tactic

jokes.text: Lionel Hutz, attorney. I'll be defending you on the charge of... murder one! Wow. Even if I lose I'll be famous.
jokes.setup: Hutz introduces himself as Bart's lawyer
jokes.punchline: Bart's excitement about fame even if convicted of murder
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's twisted priorities and fame obsession

jokes.text: That Simpson boy is looking at 180 years. Thank God we live in a country so hysterical over crime that a child can be tried as an adult.
jokes.setup: News report about Bart's case
jokes.punchline: Satirical comment on justice system treating children as adults
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary on criminal justice system's treatment of juveniles

jokes.text: Then who is the kingpin, the capo di tutti capi? That's the guy. Hey! Forgive me, Don Bartholomew.
jokes.setup: Court trying to identify the crime boss
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony identifies Bart as the don, uses formal mob title
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Bart as crime boss with formal Italian mob terminology

jokes.text: You've been his father for ten years. Do you really think he could be the leader of a murderous criminal syndicate? Well, not the leader... I mean... Oh, it's true, it's true! All the pieces fit.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asks Homer if Bart could really be a crime boss
jokes.punchline: Homer starts to defend Bart then convinces himself it's true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic spiral - starts defending son but talks himself into believing accusations

jokes.text: I made a game of seeing how many times I could bounce the ball, and breaking that record.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining how he stayed sane while trapped
jokes.punchline: References the same envelope-licking game he gave Bart
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Skinner's envelope game - he uses his own advice to survive

jokes.text: Princess Opal? I'm afraid it's splitsville for Delta Burke and Major Dad. They seemed so happy.
jokes.setup: Psychic continues with random predictions during search
jokes.punchline: Random 90s celebrity couple breakup prediction, Wiggum's concern
jokes.explanation: Continuation of fake psychic routine, dated celebrity references

jokes.text: Get bent. I deserve that.
jokes.setup: Skinner says he hopes there are no hard feelings
jokes.punchline: Bart's rude response and Skinner's acceptance of it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing relationship dynamic and Skinner's self-awareness

jokes.text: I used to think your gang was cool... but now I learned that crime doesn't pay. / Yeah, you're right.
jokes.setup: Bart delivers the moral lesson about crime not paying
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony's casual agreement despite being a successful criminal
jokes.explanation: Ironic moral lesson - Fat Tony agrees crime doesn't pay while clearly being successful at it

jokes.text: Blood on the Blackboard: The Bart Simpson Story starring Richard Chamberlain as Principal Skinner Joe Mantegna as Fat Tony Jane Seymour as the woman he loved and Neil Patrick Harris as Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: TV movie about Bart's story
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous casting choices and generic TV movie title
jokes.explanation: Good satire of exploitative TV movies with absurd casting

jokes.text: Where do you want it, Skinner? Not smart.
jokes.setup: TV movie scene showing violent confrontation
jokes.punchline: Dramatic threat followed by understatement
jokes.explanation: Generic TV movie dialogue, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: When do we get the check for this? They changed it just enough so they don't have to pay us. You know who the real crooks are-- those sleazy Hollywood producers.
jokes.setup: Family watching the TV movie about themselves
jokes.punchline: Homer's commentary on Hollywood exploitation practices
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on Hollywood's exploitation of real stories, good ending note

tmdb_overview: While working for Fat Tony, Bart gains enough influence with his boss to solve his problems with Principal Skinner--perhaps permanently.

guest_stars.name: Phil Hartman
guest_stars.character: Troy McClure / Joey / Godfather / Lionel Hutz (voice)

insight: Mob comedy grounds absurdist humor with 54 jokes in 22 minutes, anchored by Bart's noir deadpan.
